item.title: The surrender
item.description: Michael Ramirez cartoon on the war in Iran and Operation Epic Fury.

item.title: Here’s how to tell if Trump’s threadbare Iran deal is holding
item.description: The ceasefire ‘memorandum of understanding’ is vague, and Tehran knows the president’s choke point.

item.title: Removing civil servants can’t be as easy as Trump wants it to be
item.description: Civil-service reform and telework, in readers' eyes.

item.title: Trump’s big, beautiful brawl was the worst birthday present ever
item.description: A $60 million South Lawn cage match cheapened the People’s House.

item.title: The Democratic Socialists of America won’t be pacified
item.description: From Platner to Raman, a left-wing movement is poised to remake a national party in its own image.

item.title: Spielberg’s ‘Disclosure Day’ is a blockbuster argument against cynicism
item.description: The legendary director has made a film with the energy of youth and wisdom of a lifetime.

item.title: What retirement revealed about my father
item.description: I blamed him for what he couldn't fix. Now I see he’s extraordinary.

item.title: The Hoover Dam let energy flow. Congress should take the hint.
item.description: The National Environmental Policy Act is the largest hurdle to energy prosperity.

item.title: The feral hogs ravaging America could come to your home next
item.description: Money alone won’t win the bipartisan war on feral hogs.

item.title: Remember Ebola?
item.description: The Congo outbreak is far from contained.

item.title: ‘Luigi: The Musical’ is a fever dream from hell
item.description: The show isn’t meant to “glorify violence,” but it does. The protesters outside, meanwhile, think it doesn’t go far enough.

item.title: Republicans thought the Senate map was their friend. It isn’t anymore.
item.description: Polls now show Democrats competitive in 10 key races — and Trump’s numbers explain why.

item.title: The American West is burning. This maze provides kindling.
item.description: Rules and regulations block a bipartisan effort to manage American forests.

item.title: Americans condemn Nixon’s war on drugs. If only they knew the truth.
item.description: With opioids ravaging communities, the U.S. could learn from this 1971 policy.

item.title: Is there such a thing as too much empathy?
item.description: Is empathy destroying the West? That's the provocative claim at the center of Gad Saad's new book, "Suicidal Empathy." Host Megan McArdle is joined by Saad, a professor at the University of Mississippi, to discuss the trade-offs societies make in the name of compassion and whether America has the capacity to absorb differences.

item.title: What a 150-year-old oak teaches about Juneteenth
item.description: A federal holiday survives, but a national custom has yet to take root.

item.title: This absurd Social Security plan would take down progressives with it
item.description: Bernie Sanders wants a massive tax hike for the wrong priorities.
